title: Probability Distribution Selection Guide
description: A flowchart that guides the user to the appropriate probability distribution based on the characteristics of their random variable and the problem context.
relationships: Start by asking 'Is the random variable discrete or continuous?'. For discrete variables, subsequent questions lead to Binomial (fixed trials, success/failure), Geometric (waiting for first success), or Poisson (events in fixed interval). For continuous variables, questions lead to Uniform (equal probability over interval), Exponential (waiting time for events), or Normal (natural phenomena with symmetry).
